DEMURRER to Amended PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Mar-09-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENTS CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE and ROB BONTA'S DEMURRER to Amended PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION.

Defendants demur to the second amended complaint (SAC). "The basis for the demurrer is that (1) the Fourth Cause of Action does not state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action" and "(2) the First and Second Causes of Action were previously dismissed without leave to amend, which dismissal was affirmed by the Court of Appeal" (Dmr. 1:28-2:5,)

Plaintiffs "agree that the Court may dismiss" the SAC's first and second "causes of action." (Opp. 9:18-20.) It is so ordered.

While their SAC labels a "Fourth Cause of Action," plaintiffs rightly point out that this "count" combines with the SAC's "Third Cause of Action" count to assert the same "primary right," so the two may be viewed together as one cause of action. (See Skrbina v. Fleming Companies (1996) 45 Cal.App.4th 1353, 1364-65; 5 Witkin, California Procedure (6th ed. (2021) sec. 954.) Further, a "demurrer must dispose of an entire cause of action to be sustained." (Fremont Indemnity Co. v. Fremont General Corp. (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 97, 119.) Defendants' demurrer to the "Fourth Cause of Action"/count is thus overruled. The parties' other disputes can be litigated.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

DEMURRER to Amended PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Feb-07-2022 Continued To Mar-09-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Second Amended Petition/Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-16-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Michael Risher, Esq. (510.689.1657; michael@risherlaw.com) of Law Office of Michael T. Risher for Petitioners.
Avi Frey, Esq. (646.229.4385; afrey@aclunc.com) of American Civil Liberties Union Foundation of Northern California for Petitioners.
Jose Zelidon-Zepeda, Esq. (415.510.3879; jose.zelidonzepeda@doj.ca.gov) Deputy Attorney General for Respondents.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONERS CENTER FOR GENETICS AND SOCIETY, EQUAL JUSTICE SOCIETY, PETE SHANKS' Motion To File Second Amended Petition/Complaint:

Plaintiffs' motion to file second amended petition/complaint is granted. After a reversal on appeal, the trial court has the discretion to hear and determine motions to amend the pleadings. (Clark v. Baxter Healthcare Corp. (2000) 83 Cal.App.4th 1048, 1055-1056; see also Dye v. Caterpillar, Inc. (2011) 195 Cal.App.4th 1366.) The court notes that Plaintiffs previously declined to seek leave to amend, as the Court of Appeal observed. (See slip op. at 8 [Plaintiffs "disclaimed any intention to amend their claims"]). The court further notes that Plaintiffs' proposed amendment regarding standing appears superfluous in light of the Court of Appeal's finding that plaintiffs have sufficiently alleged public-interest mandamus standing for their remaining claim challenging the expungement provisions of the DNA Law. (Slip op. at 22-23.) Nevertheless, Defendants have not shown that they would be prejudiced by the proposed amendments, and the motion is supported by the liberal policy favoring amendment of pleadings. (See, e.g., Howard v. County of San Diego (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1422, 1428.) If Defendants contend that the proposed new cause of action does not state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action, they may demur after meeting and conferring as required by Code Civ. Proc. sec. 430.41.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
